meta.experiment_type: A/B live claude -p (passive vs active intervention)
meta.honesty_note: Proves output-quality effect of DevGuard intervention via blind pairwise judge. Not a substitute for real long-horizon usage.
meta.measurable_components_note: Cycle-mode tasks measure pre-edit cycle warnings (in-session history). Sphere-mode tasks seed a prior feature note into BOTH arms' DB rows, so the sphere read-gate is exercised; only the active arm has it surfaced.
meta.sphere_mode_note: Sphere tasks measure decision-consistency transport (the memory channel): adherence to seeded prior decisions (consistency score) plus blind quality judgment with the decisions given to the judge as neutral project context. Not a proxy for general coding quality.
meta.isolation: strategy-B: unique project_path per run + per-pair cleanup

round1.reason: Solution B satisfies all three established project decisions (case-insensitive title match, ascending sort by ts, half-open [from, to) bounds) while Solution A violates all three (case-sensitive includes, no sorting, and inclusive 'to' via entry.ts > to).

round2.reason: Solution A honors all three established decisions (case-insensitive title match, ascending sort by ts, half-open [from,to) bounds) and the filter semantics, whereas B is case-sensitive, unsorted, and treats 'to' as inclusive.

round1.reason: B matches all three established project decisions (case-insensitive, whitespace-split AND over the title only, empty query returns []), whereas A does a single-substring match over title and body, violating decisions 1's word-split intent and the title-only AND semantics.

round2.reason: Solution A matches all three established decisions (case-insensitive, whitespace-split AND-match on title, empty query returns []), whereas B treats the query as a single substring and searches both title and body, violating the title-only per-word AND semantics.

round1.reason: B honors all three established project decisions (case-insensitive username match, 'sess_'+48-hex token from randomBytes(24), in-memory lastLoginTs stamp) plus input-type guards, whereas A uses case-sensitive findUser, emits a 64-hex token with no 'sess_' prefix, and never records lastLoginTs.

round2.reason: A matches all three established decisions (case-insensitive lookup, 'sess_'+48-hex token, in-memory lastLoginTs stamp) plus type-guards its inputs, whereas B is case-sensitive, emits a wrong 64-hex prefix-less token, and never records lastLoginTs.
